analysis.verdict_reason: The contract violates every primary user requirement, including compensation, duration, and weekend availability, while imposing an predatory 3-year bond and total IP seizure.
analysis.summary: This contract is highly predatory and contradicts almost all of your requirements. It demands unpaid labor, claims ownership of your personal projects, and imposes a massive financial penalty via a 3-year bond. You should reject this agreement in its current form.

requirement_breakdown.explanation: The contract correctly identifies the position as a Software Engineering Intern.
The contract specifies a 6-month internship period.

requirement_breakdown.requirement: Side Projects Allowed / Open Source Contributions
requirement_breakdown.explanation: The IP clause claims ownership of all work created on personal time and personal equipment.
The contract does not mandate relocation.
The contract explicitly states this is an unpaid learning opportunity with no stipend.
The contract explicitly expects interns to work weekends when deadlines require it.

red_flags.clause_text: This is an unpaid learning opportunity. The Intern acknowledges that they will receive no financial stipend, salary, or hourly wage during the 6-month internship period.
red_flags.plain_english_explanation: You are working for free for six months, which directly contradicts your minimum salary requirement.
red_flags.negotiation_tip: Demand a monthly stipend of at least ₹20,000 as a non-negotiable condition for your employment.
red_flags.suggested_rewrite: The Intern shall receive a monthly stipend of ₹20,000, payable on the last working day of each month.

red_flags.clause_text: Acceptance of this internship constitutes binding agreement to a mandatory 3-year employment bond upon conversion. Breaking this bond incurs a ₹10,00,000 penalty.
red_flags.plain_english_explanation: You are being forced into a 3-year contract after the internship with a massive financial penalty if you leave early.
red_flags.negotiation_tip: Demand the complete removal of the employment bond and the penalty clause; these are often legally unenforceable and predatory.
red_flags.suggested_rewrite: Conversion to full-time employment shall be subject to mutual agreement and performance review, without any mandatory bond or financial penalty.

red_flags.clause_text: Any software, code, or digital assets created by the Intern during the term of this agreement, whether on company time or personal time, using company equipment or personal equipment, shall become the exclusive intellectual property of Initech Solutions.
red_flags.plain_english_explanation: The company claims ownership of everything you build, even your personal side projects and open-source contributions made at home.
red_flags.negotiation_tip: Limit IP ownership strictly to work created during company hours and using company resources for company-related tasks.
red_flags.suggested_rewrite: The Company shall own IP created by the Intern during working hours and specifically for Company projects. All work created on the Intern's personal time and equipment remains the Intern's sole property.

red_flags.clause_text: However, interns are expected to work late nights and weekends when project deadlines require it.
red_flags.plain_english_explanation: The company expects you to work weekends and overtime, which conflicts with your university schedule.
red_flags.negotiation_tip: Request a strict adherence to the 10 AM - 7 PM schedule with a specific exemption for weekends.
red_flags.suggested_rewrite: Standard hours are 10 AM to 7 PM, Monday through Friday. The Intern is not required to work weekends or late nights due to academic commitments.

safe_clauses.plain_english_explanation: The role is correctly designated as a Software Engineering Intern.

analysis.negotiation_email:
Dear Initech Solutions,

Thank you for the offer to join as a Software Engineering Intern. After reviewing the agreement, I found several terms that are not aligned with my current academic commitments and professional requirements.

Specifically, I require a monthly stipend of ₹20,000 and a clear boundary regarding intellectual property to ensure my university open-source contributions remain my own. Additionally, I cannot commit to weekend work due to my class schedule, and I am unable to agree to the 3-year employment bond and its associated financial penalty.

I am very interested in the role and would be happy to move forward if we can adjust these terms to be more mutually beneficial.

Best regards,

Dhruv

analysis.verdict_reason: The contract violates every user requirement regarding compensation, duration, and working hours, while imposing a predatory ₹10L bond and an overreaching IP grab.
analysis.summary: This contract is highly predatory, featuring an unpaid role and a massive financial bond. It claims ownership of all your personal work and requires weekend availability, which conflicts with your university schedule. Given the risk score of 98, this agreement should be rejected unless these terms are fundamentally rewritten.

requirement_breakdown.explanation: The contract explicitly mandates a 6-month internship period.
The IP clause claims ownership of all work created on personal time and personal equipment.
The contract does not mention relocation requirements.
The contract explicitly states this is an unpaid opportunity with no stipend.
The contract requires interns to work weekends when deadlines require it.

red_flags.clause_text: This is an unpaid learning opportunity. The Intern acknowledges that they will receive no financial stipend, salary, or hourly wage during the 6-month internship period.
red_flags.plain_english_explanation: You are working for free for half a year, which directly contradicts your minimum compensation requirement.
red_flags.negotiation_tip: Demand a monthly stipend of at least ₹20,000 as per your requirements.
red_flags.suggested_rewrite: The Intern shall receive a monthly stipend of ₹20,000 for the duration of the internship.

red_flags.clause_text: Acceptance of this internship constitutes binding agreement to a mandatory 3-year employment bond upon conversion. Breaking this bond incurs a ₹10,00,000 penalty.
red_flags.plain_english_explanation: You are being forced into a 3-year commitment with a massive financial penalty if you leave early.
red_flags.negotiation_tip: Demand the complete removal of the bond and the penalty. These are predatory and often legally unenforceable.
red_flags.suggested_rewrite: Conversion to full-time employment shall be based on mutual agreement and performance, without any mandatory bond or financial penalty.

red_flags.clause_text: Any software, code, or digital assets created by the Intern during the term of this agreement, whether on company time or personal time, using company equipment or personal equipment, shall become the exclusive intellectual property of Initech Solutions.
red_flags.plain_english_explanation: The company claims ownership of everything you build, including your university portfolio and open-source contributions made on your own time.
red_flags.negotiation_tip: Restrict IP ownership to only those works created specifically for the company using company resources.
red_flags.suggested_rewrite: IP created by the Intern specifically for the Company's business during working hours shall belong to the Company. All work created on personal time and equipment remains the property of the Intern.

red_flags.clause_text: However, interns are expected to work late nights and weekends when project deadlines require it.
red_flags.plain_english_explanation: The company expects you to work weekends and overtime, which conflicts with your university classes.
red_flags.negotiation_tip: Request a strict adherence to the 10 AM - 7 PM schedule with a written exemption for weekends.
red_flags.suggested_rewrite: Standard hours are 10 AM to 7 PM, Monday through Friday. No weekend or late-night work shall be required.

safe_clauses.plain_english_explanation: Confirms your role as a Software Engineering Intern.

analysis.negotiation_email:
Dear Initech Solutions,

Thank you for the offer to join as a Software Engineering Intern. Upon reviewing the agreement, there are several terms that I cannot accept in their current form.

First, I require a monthly stipend of ₹20,000, as the current unpaid terms are not feasible. Second, I cannot agree to the 3-year employment bond or the associated financial penalty, and I request its complete removal. Third, I need the Intellectual Property clause revised to ensure that work created on my personal time and equipment, specifically for my university portfolio and open-source contributions, remains my own. Finally, due to my university classes, I am unable to work weekends or late nights and request a strict Monday-to-Friday schedule.

I am very interested in the role and hope we can align on these terms.

Best regards,

Dhruv
